title: Quadratic families of elliptic curves and unirationality of degree 1 conic bundles
speaker: János Kollár
abstract: We consider elliptic curves whose coefficients are degree 2 polynomials in a variable $t$. We prove that for infinitely many values of $t$ the resulting elliptic curve has rank at least 1. All such curves together form an algebraic surface which is birational to a conic bundle with 7 singular fibers. The main step of the proof is to show that such conic bundles are unirational. (joint work with M. Mella)

proof_stages.label: Quadratic elliptic families and the arithmetic target
proof_stages.summary: The talk sets up non-isotrivial one-parameter families of elliptic curves with coefficient polynomials of degree at most two and states the corollary that many parameter values give infinitely many rational points.

proof_stages.label: Algebraic-geometric reformulation as a conic bundle
proof_stages.summary: The family is assembled into a surface, and the quadratic dependence on the parameter makes the surface a conic bundle over the x-line, typically with six finite singular fibers plus one at infinity.

proof_stages.label: Unirationality criterion for conic bundles with at most seven singular fibers
proof_stages.summary: The central geometric theorem is stated: over a field of characteristic not two, a conic bundle with at most seven singular fibers is unirational exactly when it has a rational point, and the seven-fiber case always supplies such a point via the anticanonical pencil.

proof_stages.label: Weak del Pezzo geometry and the Bertini involution
proof_stages.summary: The proof analyzes the five-, six-, and seven-fiber weak del Pezzo models, introduces the unique base point p* of the anticanonical pencil, and distinguishes the general and special seven-fiber normal forms by comparing the Bertini involution with the double-cover involution.

proof_stages.label: Enriques criterion and the general seven-fiber case
proof_stages.summary: Using the criterion that a conic bundle is unirational iff it has a rational multisection, the smooth distinguished fiber through p* is sent by the Bertini involution to a degree-eight multisection in the general case.

proof_stages.label: Special-case obstruction and elliptic multiplication maps
proof_stages.summary: In the special case the distinguished fiber may be nonrational and Bertini-invariant, so the speaker tries multiplication-by-2 or multiplication-by-3 maps in the elliptic fibration, obtaining a partial criterion from a non-six-torsion rational point but still needing a way to create such points.

proof_stages.label: Multisecant descent maps for degree-one conic bundles
proof_stages.summary: Motivated by secant descent on cubic surfaces, the proof constructs for seven-fiber conic bundles multisecant maps from symmetric powers by showing that |-K+nF| with imposed double base points has a unique residual base point, allowing unirationality to descend from a bounded field extension.

proof_stages.label: Economical degree-two modification and explicit equations
proof_stages.summary: After the abstract proof, the speaker outlines a smaller n=2 construction using conjugate points on a conic fiber, elliptic doubling, elementary transformations of the conic bundle, and an explicit system of polynomial equations for the resulting rational curve.
